lessons.title: Factual Causation — The But For Test
lessons.description: The primary test for factual causation is the but for test: but for the defendant's breach of duty, would the plaintiff have suffered the same damage?

lessons.title: Novus Actus Interveniens
lessons.description: The concept of novus actus interveniens reflects the principle that the law cannot hold a defendant responsible indefinitely for all consequences that flow from their breach.

lessons.title: Material Contribution and Complex Causation
lessons.description: We established in Lesson 4.1 that where the but for test cannot be strictly satisfied — because of multiple causes — the material contribution test may bridge the gap.
